title: Donnellsmithia pinnatisecta
taxonomy: Donnellsmithia pinnatisecta is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is native to Mexico and has been classified as a member of the genus Donnellsmithia, a group of plants that are often referred to as the "Mexican mustard" or "Mexican cabbage" plants. This classification is based on its physical characteristics, including its pinnate leaves and small, yellow flowers. The plant is further classified as a member of the tribe Brassiceae, a group of plants that are known for their small, four-petaled flowers. By classification, Donnellsmithia pinnatisecta is a dicotyledonous plant, meaning it has two cotyledons or seed leaves in its seedling stage.
